beats.reasoning: A calm morning routine to match Marcus’s hyper-focused, chatty-but-prepped energy.

beats.reasoning: Sunny mornings encourage an easy grab-and-go start.

beats.activity: Drives toward Kirkland and settles in with a podcast, arriving with time to spare.
beats.reasoning: Totem Lake is his workplace area; driving is straightforward given light rail maintenance.

diary:
This morning started easy—coffee going, shower done, and that little mental checklist I always run before I leave. Woodinville felt bright and calm, like the whole day was already smiling at me. I grabbed a snack and more caffeine at Eagle’s Coffee, then rolled toward Totem Lake with my podcast queued up. Work was steady at the school—Totem Lake again—rounds of check-ins, a couple parent calls, and me trying to figure out what “small support” actually looks like for each kid without making it feel like a big deal.

By lunchtime at Paseo I was laughing with a coworker, then I did a quick supply stop at QFC and later picked up that film roll at Fry’s Electronics. After lunch I wrote some letters by hand at home, organized my photo gear, and felt my shoulders unclench. When the USA vs Wales match kicked off at Tavern Hall on Capitol Hill, it was pure joy—crowd energy, cheering, strangers talking like friends. Afterward at Pike Brewing, I stayed longer than I planned. Now I’m home again, board games set out for tomorrow, and I feel quietly good—like I earned the calm.
thought_process: I tried to move through the day with low-friction choices: breakfast on the way, podcast for the drive, and quick errands clustered around the times my brain felt most functional. At school, I focused on check-ins and notes first because that’s where the momentum lives—if I get the early conversations right, the rest of the week feels less scattered. The light rail closure didn’t hit me directly since I drove, but I still felt a tiny awareness of the city’s altered rhythm, like everyone was rerouting their routines. The World Cup match absolutely softened my mood; I could feel my stress turning into something social and easy during the USA vs Wales game. Even writing letters afterward felt like a continuation of that—slow, deliberate, and kind. By evening, I was tired but satisfied.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
